title: Agents That Choose Their Own Retrieval Tools
concept: An agent loop selecting between multiple MCP-exposed retrieval sources based on the query at hand.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary benefit of exposing a RAG pipeline as an MCP Resource/Tool?

options.text: It allows any compliant client application to query the RAG pipeline as a standard tool.
It replaces the vector database with a traditional relational database.
It requires the LLM to be retrained on local text files from scratch.

exercises.explain: In the previous lesson, we learned that MCP allows a RAG pipeline to be packaged as a tool, allowing any client application to easily query it using a standard protocol.

cards.title: Too Many Choices?
cards.body: Imagine having ten different databases: customer feedback, code repositories, legal contracts, and more. If you dump them all into one search, you get noisy results. What if your AI could pause, think, and pick the single best tool for the job?

cards.title: Agentic Retrieval
cards.body: An Agentic Retrieval system uses a LLM decision loop to analyze a query and select the most relevant retrieval tool from multiple options exposed via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Instead of searching everything, it only calls the specific source it needs.

cards.title: The Smart Office Manager
cards.body: Think of an AI agent as a busy office manager. Instead of reading every file folder in the cabinet for every question, the manager reads the client's request first, decides whether to open the 'Financial Ledger' tool or the 'Customer Reviews' tool, and only pulls the information they actually need.

cards.title: Choosing the Financial Tool
cards.body: If a user asks 'What was our revenue in Q3?', the agent loop bypasses the 'HR Handbook' MCP tool and calls the 'Financial DB Query' MCP tool, retrieving only the relevant spreadsheet data to answer.

cards.title: More Databases Aren't Always Better
cards.body: People often think agents must search every connected database to give a complete answer. In reality, querying too many sources wastes time and introduces irrelevant noise; choosing a single targeted tool is much faster and more accurate.

cards.title: Static vs. Agentic Selection
cards.body: Comparing static RAG (which searches a single predefined database) with agentic retrieval (which dynamically selects the correct database using MCP).

left.text: Queries one fixed index for every single question.

right.text: Selects between Legal, Code, or Finance tools dynamically.

exercises.prompt: How does an agent decide which MCP retrieval tool to use?

options.text: It hardcodes the search to always query the first database in the list.
It uses LLM reasoning to choose the most relevant MCP tool based on the user's question.
It merges all databases into one single giant text file before searching.
It asks the user to manually type the exact SQL query database name every time.

exercises.explain: By analyzing the query beforehand, the LLM agent can select the most appropriate tool, saving time and reducing irrelevant data in the prompt context.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An agent loop can choose to call multiple different MCP tools in a row if a query requires info from different sources.
exercises.explain: An agent can choose to call zero, one, or multiple tools in sequence depending on how complex the user's prompt is.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing component that decides which MCP tool to run.
exercises.sentence: The agent loop evaluates the user request and selects the correct tool.
exercises.explain: The agent loop is the core decision-making cycle where the AI evaluates the query and chooses which MCP tool to run.

exercises.prompt: Identify the output generated by an agent deciding between retrieval tools:

options.text: "The user wants code examples. I should call the 'github_repo_search' tool instead of the 'medical_records' tool. Calling tool now..."
SELECT * FROM documents WHERE text LIKE '%finance%';

exercises.explain: Option B represents an AI agent systematically deciding which specific tool to invoke based on a semantic query, which is a classic agentic retrieval loop pattern. The others are static code behaviors.

exercises.prompt: How does an agent loop successfully handle a complex, multi-database query using MCP?

options.text: The agent queries all available databases simultaneously and merges the raw text before looking at the user's query.
The agent evaluates the query first, selects the specific MCP tool designed for that domain, retrieves the context, and then answers.
The MCP host automatically blocks the agent from choosing tools to prevent security risks.
The agent bypasses the tools and generates a hallucinated answer to save API tokens.

exercises.explain: An agent loop acts as a decision-maker. It evaluates the user's query, checks its available MCP tools (each representing a different RAG pipeline), selects the correct one to call, and then synthesizes the retrieved data into a final answer.
